trigger_reason: Retrospective application of v12 step 05.5 mechanical anti-R279 structural filter to R279's existing 05_candidate.json. R279 PTCH (Pan-Tuned Concept Heads) is the canonical R279-pattern candidate (the eponymous case). v12 step 05.5 retrofit validates that the classifier correctly identifies the namesake.

candidate_classifier_input.llm_application: PTCH (Pan-Tuned Concept Heads): a multi-head attention fine-tuning rule that constrains each head's top-K singular directions (a fundamental + secondary 'overtones') to integer-ratio multiples in the embedding space, regularized by a harmonic-alignment loss; inter-head 'grooves' enforce orthogonality. Each head becomes a single 'note' with explicit harmonic structure.
sub_mechanisms_from_v7_token_streams: M_1 within-head SVD on attention weights
M_2 top-K singular directions per head
M_3 integer-ratio target spectrum (the distinctive piece)

structural_questions.Q1_evidence: The llm_application does NOT introduce a new learnable module. The 'integer-ratio target spectrum' is a FIXED TARGET (not learnable parameters); the regularization is on EXISTING per-head SVD singular directions (which already exist in baseline LoRA / attention computation). No new matrix, MLP, attention sub-layer, or learnable gate is added.
structural_questions.Q2_evidence: PTCH operates WITHIN each head; the 'inter-head grooves' enforce orthogonality between existing heads (a regularization on existing pairwise relationships), not a new pathway. No new edge in the computation graph between previously-disconnected components.
structural_questions.Q3_evidence: Same head count (multi-head attention preserved), same layer count, same kernel family (softmax attention unchanged); the regularization loss adds gradient pressure but does not change architectural topology.

regeneration_attempted_note: Retrofit classification only; no regeneration applied retroactively. The classifier's job is to validate that step 05.5 correctly identifies the canonical R279-pattern candidate.
rationale: R279 PTCH is the CANONICAL R279-pattern candidate (the eponymous case). Per program_v12.md §3.1, R279's llm_application is a soft regularization (harmonic-alignment loss) toward a fixed target spectrum on EXISTING per-head SVD singular directions. Q1=NO (no new learnable module — the 'fundamental + overtone' target is FIXED, not learnable); Q2=NO (inter-head orthogonality is on EXISTING head pairs, not a new pathway); Q3=NO (architectural topology unchanged). All three questions NO → R279_pattern = TRUE → verdict = REJECTED_R279_PATTERN. This is the trivially-correct classification — R279 is the namesake R279-pattern candidate.
post_filter_candidate_used_downstream: n/a (retrofit; downstream pipeline already ran under v7/v10/v11)
honest_classification_note: Step 05.5 retrofit main-context-direct (deterministic classifier on R279's 05_candidate.json text); no Agent spawn. The classification confirms what v7 step 11.5 + v10 step 13 + v11 step 13.5 found through three orthogonal evidence channels: R279 = PARAMETRIZATION-ONLY (= R279-pattern at the structural level).

v7_v10_v11_v12_convergence.v7_step_11_5_finding: SORSA/SODA at functional similarity 0.80 covers M_1+M_2+M_4+M_5 (literature evidence)
v7_v10_v11_v12_convergence.v10_step_13_finding: Distinguishability_pre_check = false (structural-reasoning evidence — M_3 alone differs, collapses under SVD geometry pressure)
v7_v10_v11_v12_convergence.v11_step_13_5_finding: Adversarial attack: 4/4 attacks succeeded across 4 categories (adversarial-empirical evidence)
v7_v10_v11_v12_convergence.v12_step_05_5_finding: R279_pattern = TRUE; Q1=Q2=Q3=NO (structural-classifier evidence — no new module, no new connection, no topology change)
v7_v10_v11_v12_convergence.convergence: FOUR independent channels (v7 lit / v10 struct / v11 adv-empirical / v12 structural-classifier) all converge on R279 = PARAMETRIZATION-ONLY = R279-pattern. v12 adds the fourth orthogonal signal — a mechanical pre-search classification that would have rejected R279 at step 05.5 (saving steps 06-13.5 budget) if v12 had been operational at R279's original execution.

v12_diagnostic_value: The R279 retrofit-classifier demonstrates step 05.5's correct behavior on the canonical R279-pattern case. If step 05.5 had been in place at R279's original execution, the candidate would have been rejected pre-search and regenerated; the corpus would have been spared the R279 retrofit-discovery loop entirely. This is the v12 channel's preventive value — catching R279-pattern BEFORE the expensive verifier chain runs.
